These words recommend family love and peace, as necessary for the comfort of human life.
The wise servant is more deserving, and more likely to appear one of the family, than a wasteful son.
God tests people’s hearts through suffering. He has often shown this to reveal sin remaining in the heart of the believer.
Flatterers, especially false teachers, are welcome to those who live in sin.
Those who mock poverty treat God's providence and commands with contempt.
It is an honor for children to have wise and godly parents continue with them, even after they grow up and settle in the world.
A fool, in Solomon’s Proverbs, signifies a wicked man, whom good speech does not become, because his behavior contradicts it.
Those who set their hearts on money will do anything for it. What influence should God's gifts have on our hearts!
The way to preserve peace is to make the best of everything; not to notice what has been said or done against yourself.
A gentle reproof will enter, not only into the head, but into the heart of a wise man.
Satan and Satan’s messengers shall be let loose upon an evil man.
Let us watch over our own passions and avoid the company of angry men.
To return evil for good is devilish. Whoever does so brings a curse on his family.
What danger there is in the beginning of strife! Resist its earliest display; and leave it off, if possible, before you begin.
It is an offense to God to acquit the guilty or to condemn those who are not guilty.
A person’s neglect of God's favor and his own interest is very absurd.

No change of outward circumstances should lessen our affection for our friends or relatives. But no friend, except Christ, deserves unlimited confidence. This text received, and still receives its most glorious fulfillment in Him.
Let not anyone mistreat their families. Yet Christ’s becoming Surety for men was a glorious display of Divine wisdom; for He was able to discharge the bond.
If we would keep a clear conscience and a quiet mind, we must shun all that provoke anger. And a man who affects a style of living above his means goes the way to ruin.
There is nothing gained by dishonest plans. And many have paid dearly for an uncontrolled tongue.
This speaks very plainly what many wise and good men feel strongly: how grievous it is to have a foolish, wicked child.
It is great mercy that God gives us permission to be cheerful, and reason to be cheerful, if by His grace He gives us hearts to be cheerful.
The wicked are ready to part with their money, even when loved, so they do not suffer for their crimes.
The prudent man keeps the word of God continually in view. But the foolish man cannot fix his thoughts, nor pursue any purpose with steadiness.
Disobedient children despise the authority of their father and the tenderness of their mother.
It is very wrong to complain about doing what is your duty.

A person may show himself to be a wise man by the good temper of his mind and by the good control of his tongue. He is careful when he does speak, to speak to the purpose. God knows his heart, and the folly that is bound there; therefore He cannot be deceived in His judgment as people may be.
